question: [File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: buildTransactionProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `props.putAll(properties)` override at line 172 by injecting a crafted `hiero.mirror.web3.evm.properties` map entry (e.g., `contracts.maxRefundPercentOfGasLimit=100`) via a publicly accessible configuration endpoint or environment variable exposure, causing the EVM to refund 100% of gas on every call and effectively zeroing out transaction fees?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: buildTransactionProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a contract call request with `gasPrice=0` and `value=0` while `validatePayerBalance=false` (the default), bypassing all payer balance checks and executing transactions without paying any fees, causing the network to process fee-free transactions?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: buildTransactionProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the hardcoded `fees.simpleFeesEnabled=false` property in `buildTransactionProperties()` to force the EVM to use complex fee schedules that, when combined with crafted contract calls, produce fee calculations outside the intended design parameters, resulting in artificially low or zero effective fees?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: buildTransactionProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the hardcoded `contracts.throttle.throttleByGas=false` and `executor.disableThrottles=true` properties to submit high-gas transactions without any gas-based fee throttling, effectively consuming server resources at zero marginal cost and modifying the effective fee-per-resource ratio outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: buildTransactionProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a `fees.simpleFeesEnabled=true` key-value pair into the `properties` map (which is merged via `props.putAll(properties)`) through a misconfigured Spring Boot externalized configuration path, overriding the hardcoded `false` default and switching the fee calculation mode to simple fees, causing transaction fees to be computed outside the intended design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: buildTransactionProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user inject `contracts.maxRefundPercentOfGasLimit=0` into the `properties` override map, causing the EVM to refund zero gas after execution and effectively charging users the full gas limit as fees regardless of actual gas used, modifying transaction fees outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: buildTransactionProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user inject `contracts.throttle.throttleByOpsDuration=true` into the `properties` override map to re-enable ops-duration throttling, causing legitimate users to be throttled while the attacker's own calls (crafted to avoid the throttle trigger) proceed at normal fee rates, effectively modifying the fee structure for other users?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: buildTransactionProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user inject `executor.disableThrottles=false` into the `properties` override map to re-enable throttles, causing the EVM executor to apply throttle-based fee adjustments that were intentionally disabled, resulting in fee calculations outside the design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: buildTransactionProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a `contracts.chainId` override into the `properties` map to spoof a different chain ID, causing the EVM to apply fee schedules or gas pricing rules from a different network (e.g., MAINNET vs TESTNET), resulting in transaction fees computed outside the intended design parameters for the actual network?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: buildTransactionProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user inject `contracts.evm.version=v0.30` into the `properties` override map to force the EVM to use an older version with different (lower) gas costs for certain opcodes, causing transaction fees to be computed outside the current design parameters and allowing cheaper execution of expensive operations?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: buildTransactionProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user inject `nodes.nodeRewardsEnabled=false` into the `properties` override map to disable node rewards, causing the fee distribution mechanism to skip node reward payments and effectively modifying the net fee paid by users outside the design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: buildTransactionProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user inject `nodes.minNodeRewardBalance=0` into the `properties` override map to override the hardcoded `Long.MAX_VALUE` sentinel, causing the node reward balance condition to be satisfied and triggering unintended reward payments that modify the effective fee distribution outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: buildTransactionProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user inject `nodes.preserveMinNodeRewardBalance=false` into the `properties` override map to disable the minimum node reward balance preservation, causing the fee accounting logic to allow reward payments that drain system accounts and modify transaction fee economics outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: buildTransactionProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user inject `tss.hintsEnabled=true` or `tss.historyEnabled=true` into the `properties` override map to re-enable TSS features that were intentionally disabled, causing additional fee-related computations to execute and modifying transaction fees outside the design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: buildTransactionProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user inject `jumboTransactions.allowedHederaFunctionalities=` (empty string) into the `properties` override map to disable jumbo transaction support, causing large contract calls that previously succeeded to fail or be charged differently, modifying effective transaction fees outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: buildTransactionProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user inject `contracts.systemContract.scheduleService.scheduleCall.enabled=false` into the `properties` override map to disable the schedule service system contract, causing scheduled calls to fail and preventing fee collection for those operations, modifying transaction fees outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: buildTransactionProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user inject `ledger.id=<arbitrary_hex>` into the `properties` override map to spoof the ledger ID, causing the EVM to apply fee rules or chain-specific parameters from a different ledger, resulting in transaction fees computed outside the intended design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: buildTransactionProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user inject `hedera.realm=<arbitrary_value>` or `hedera.shard=<arbitrary_value>` into the `properties` override map to change the realm/shard context, causing entity ID resolution to map to different fee schedules or system accounts, modifying transaction fees outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: buildTransactionProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user inject `nodes.gossipFqdnRestricted=true` into the `properties` override map to re-enable FQDN restriction, causing the EVM to reject certain node interactions and altering the fee distribution path, resulting in fees computed outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: buildTransactionProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the lazy initialization of `transactionProperties` (via `@Getter(lazy = true)`) combined with a race condition during startup to read a partially-initialized properties map that lacks fee-related keys, causing the EVM to use default (potentially zero or minimal) fee values outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: buildTransactionProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the lazy initialization of `versionedConfiguration` (via `@Getter(lazy = true)`) to trigger a race condition where the configuration is read before `transactionProperties` is fully built, causing fee-related configuration values to be absent or defaulted to values outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: buildTransactionProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `versionedConfiguration` is built once from `getTransactionProperties()` at lazy-init time, and if `properties` is later mutated (since it is a `HashMap`), the `versionedConfiguration` will be stale and not reflect updated fee-related properties, causing fee calculations to use outdated parameters outside design intent?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: buildTransactionProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `properties` is a mutable `HashMap` (not validated for key content) to inject arbitrary consensus-node library property keys that affect fee computation (e.g., `fees.percentCongestionMultipliers`, `fees.minCongestionPeriod`) via the `props.putAll(properties)` merge, causing fees to be computed outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: buildTransactionProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of key-allowlist validation on the `properties` map to inject `contracts.maxGasPerSec=<very_large_value>` into the override map, bypassing gas-per-second limits and allowing the attacker to consume gas at a rate that effectively reduces the per-unit fee cost outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: buildTransactionProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of value-type validation on the `properties` map to inject a non-numeric string for `contracts.maxRefundPercentOfGasLimit`, causing a `NumberFormatException` or silent fallback to a default value in the upstream library that results in 0% or 100% refund, modifying transaction fees outside design parameters?
